title: System Lockout — Ignition Failure
shortDescription: The furnace has locked out after failing to ignite within the allowed retry attempts.
fullDescription: One blink on a Goodman furnace indicates the system entered lockout after the maximum number of ignition retries was exceeded. The control board attempted to light the burners multiple times without detecting flame. The lockout resets automatically after about one hour or by cycling power.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately and call your gas company. Do not repeatedly reset if the furnace keeps locking out.
symptoms: One LED blink on control board
Igniter may glow but no flame

fixSteps.title: Reset the System
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off furnace power for 30 seconds, then restore. Observe the ignition cycle.

fixSteps.title: Clean Flame Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the flame sensor and clean with fine emery cloth until the metal rod is shiny.

fixSteps.title: Check Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Watch for the igniter to glow during startup. Test resistance with multimeter (10-200 ohms normal).

fixSteps.title: Verify Gas Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the manual gas shutoff valve is open. Confirm other gas appliances work.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the flame sensor and checking the igniter does not stop the lockouts, or if you suspect a gas supply issue.

faq.q: How long is the lockout?
faq.a: About 1 hour for auto-reset. You can manually reset by cycling power.

faq.q: What causes repeated lockouts?
faq.a: Usually a dirty flame sensor or weak igniter. Clean the sensor first.

faq.q: Are Goodman and Amana the same?
faq.a: Goodman and Amana are made by the same manufacturer (Daikin/Goodman) and share similar diagnostic codes.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck Open
shortDescription: The pressure switch did not close, indicating a draft or venting problem.
fullDescription: Two blinks on a Goodman furnace indicate the pressure switch failed to close after the draft inducer motor started. The pressure switch verifies proper exhaust venting before allowing gas to flow.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Never bypass the pressure switch. It prevents carbon monoxide from entering your home. If you smell gas, leave and call your gas company.
symptoms: Draft inducer runs but burners do not light

fixSteps.title: Check Vent Pipes
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect outdoor exhaust and intake pipes for ice, snow, bird nests, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Pressure Hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber tube from the pressure switch for cracks, disconnections, or trapped water.

fixSteps.title: Clear Condensate Drain
fixSteps.instruction: On high-efficiency models, flush the condensate trap and drain line.

fixSteps.title: Test Pressure Switch
fixSteps.instruction: With the inducer running, check for continuity across the switch terminals.

whenToCallPro: If vents are clear and hose is intact but the switch still does not close.

faq.q: Why does this happen in winter?
faq.a: Ice can block the outdoor vent pipes during cold weather.

faq.q: Can I jump the pressure switch?
faq.a: Never. It is a critical CO safety device.

faq.q: How much is a pressure switch?
faq.a: $20-$50 for the part; $150-$300 installed professionally.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck Closed
shortDescription: The pressure switch is closed before the draft inducer starts, indicating a stuck switch or wiring fault.
fullDescription: Three blinks indicate the pressure switch is stuck in the closed position before the inducer motor has started. This prevents the furnace from starting its normal sequence.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: A stuck-closed pressure switch is a safety concern. Do not operate the furnace until resolved.

causes.cause: Water in pressure hose creating false reading

symptoms: Error appears immediately on heat call

fixSteps.title: Drain Pressure Hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and drain the pressure switch hose. Reconnect securely.

fixSteps.title: Test Switch
fixSteps.instruction: With power off and inducer off, test for continuity. Should show open (no continuity).

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wires for shorts that could bridge the switch terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace Switch
fixSteps.instruction: If stuck closed, replace with the correct part number for your model.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot determine why the switch is stuck or if replacement does not fix it.

faq.q: Can water cause this?
faq.a: Yes, trapped water in the hose can hold the switch closed.

faq.q: Is this dangerous?
faq.a: The control board prevents operation to maintain safety. The switch needs repair.

faq.q: What part number do I need?
faq.a: Check the data plate on the old switch or look up your furnace model number.

title: Open High Limit Switch — Overheating
shortDescription: The furnace overheated and the high limit safety switch tripped.
fullDescription: Four blinks on a Goodman furnace indicate the high temperature limit switch opened due to overheating, typically caused by restricted airflow through the system.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Repeated overheating stresses the heat exchanger and can cause cracks that leak carbon monoxide. Install and maintain CO detectors.
symptoms: Furnace starts then shuts off quickly

fixSteps.title: Replace Air Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Check and replace the air filter. This is the most common cause.

fixSteps.title: Open All Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all supply and return vents are open and unblocked.

fixSteps.title: Check Blower
fixSteps.instruction: Set fan to ON at the thermostat. If the blower does not run, the motor or capacitor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Clean Blower Wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power and inspect the blower wheel for heavy dust buildup.

whenToCallPro: If the filter is clean, vents are open, and it still overheats. Blower motor or heat exchanger may need inspection.

faq.q: How often should I change the filter?
faq.a: Every 1-3 months for standard filters.

faq.q: Can closing vents cause this?
faq.a: Yes, closing too many vents restricts airflow and causes overheating.

faq.q: Is overheating bad for my furnace?
faq.a: Yes, it stresses the heat exchanger and can cause cracking over time.

title: Flame Sensed Without Gas Valve Command
shortDescription: Flame detected when the gas valve should be off.
fullDescription: Five blinks indicate the flame sensing circuit detects flame when no gas should be flowing. This is a critical safety lockout.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: May indicate a leaking gas valve. If you smell gas, evacuate and call 911 or your gas company immediately.

causes.cause: Flame sensor wire shorted to ground

fixSteps.title: Check for Gas Odor
fixSteps.instruction: If gas is detected, leave immediately and call emergency services.

fixSteps.title: Shut Down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off furnace power and gas supply valve.

fixSteps.title: Check Flame Sensor Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, look for damaged or grounded flame sensor wire.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: Gas valve issues require professional diagnosis and repair.

whenToCallPro: Immediately. This is a safety-critical code.

faq.q: Is this an emergency?
faq.a: If you smell gas, yes. Even without odor, get it inspected promptly.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: No, gas valve issues require a licensed technician.

faq.q: How much is the repair?
faq.a: Gas valve replacement typically costs $250-$700 including labor.

title: Line Voltage Polarity Reversed
shortDescription: Incorrect wiring polarity or missing ground detected.
fullDescription: Six blinks indicate the Goodman furnace control board has detected reversed hot/neutral polarity or a grounding problem.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off power at the breaker before inspecting electrical connections.
symptoms: Often follows new installation or electrical work

fixSteps.title: Turn Off Power
fixSteps.instruction: Switch off the breaker before any inspection.

fixSteps.title: Check Polarity
fixSteps.instruction: Verify hot (black) is on hot terminal and neutral (white) on neutral at the furnace power connection.

fixSteps.title: Check Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure ground wire is secure at the furnace chassis.

fixSteps.title: Test Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Use an outlet tester on the furnace power source to verify correct polarity.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable with electrical work or if correcting polarity does not fix it.

faq.q: Why does polarity matter?
faq.a: The flame sensor circuit requires correct polarity to detect flame.

faq.q: Is this common?
faq.a: Common after new installation or electrical work.

faq.q: How do I test polarity?
faq.a: Use a $10-15 outlet tester or a multimeter.

title: Gas Valve Circuit Error
shortDescription: Electrical fault in the gas valve circuit detected.
fullDescription: Seven blinks indicate a fault in the gas valve electrical circuit — open or shorted coil, wiring issue, or control board relay problem.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Do not attempt to manually open the gas valve. Call a professional for gas valve issues.
symptoms: Igniter glows but no gas flows

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off power 60 seconds, then restore.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wire connections between control board and gas valve.

fixSteps.title: Test Valve Coil
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across gas valve coil terminals. Compare to spec.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: Gas valve replacement requires a licensed technician.

whenToCallPro: For any gas valve replacement or if wiring checks do not resolve the issue.

faq.q: Can I replace the gas valve myself?
faq.a: No, gas valve work should be done by a licensed technician.

faq.q: How much is a Goodman gas valve?
faq.a: $100-$300 for the part; $250-$700 total with labor.

faq.q: Could it just be a loose wire?
faq.a: Yes, check wiring connections first before assuming the valve is bad.

title: Igniter Circuit Failure
shortDescription: The hot surface igniter circuit is open — broken igniter element.
fullDescription: Eight blinks indicate the igniter has an open circuit. The heating element is cracked or broken and cannot heat up to ignite the gas.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off furnace before handling igniter. Do not touch the element with bare hands.
symptoms: Draft inducer runs but no ignition

fixSteps.title: Visual Check
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the igniter for visible cracks.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the igniter wire connector is seated firmly.

fixSteps.title: Test Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance: 10-200 ohms is good, OL means broken.

fixSteps.title: Replace Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screw, swap igniter. Handle only by ceramic base.

whenToCallPro: If a new igniter does not resolve the issue.

faq.q: How long do igniters last?
faq.a: 3-7 years typically.

faq.q: Where do I buy a Goodman igniter?
faq.a: Use your model number to find the OEM part online or at an HVAC supplier.

faq.q: Why not touch the element?
faq.a: Skin oils create hot spots that cause premature failure.

title: Low Flame Signal
shortDescription: The flame sensor is reading a weak flame signal.
fullDescription: Nine blinks indicate the flame sensor microamp reading is below the acceptable threshold. The furnace may light briefly then shut off, or operate intermittently.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Weak combustion can produce carbon monoxide. Ensure CO detectors are functional.
symptoms: Furnace lights then shuts off in seconds

fixSteps.title: Clean Flame Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the flame sensor with fine emery cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check Insulator
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the ceramic insulator for cracks.

fixSteps.title: Observe Flames
fixSteps.instruction: Blue with small yellow tips is normal. All yellow indicates problems.

fixSteps.title: Gas Pressure Check
fixSteps.instruction: A technician should measure manifold pressure if cleaning does not help.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the sensor does not fix the issue or flames look abnormal.

faq.q: How do I clean the flame sensor?
faq.a: Remove the single mounting screw, pull out the sensor, clean the metal rod with fine emery cloth, and reinstall.

faq.q: What is a normal flame sensor reading?
faq.a: 1.5-6 microamps is typical. Below 1 microamp causes shutdowns.

faq.q: How often should I clean it?
faq.a: At least annually during maintenance.

title: Normal Operation — Power On
shortDescription: Steady LED indicates the control board has power and no active faults.
fullDescription: A steady (non-blinking) LED on a Goodman furnace control board indicates the system has power and is functioning normally. If the furnace is not heating, check the thermostat.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Even in normal standby, ensure CO detectors are installed and working.

fixSteps.title: Check Thermostat
fixSteps.instruction: Set to HEAT mode with temp above room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Check Batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Replace thermostat batteries if applicable.

fixSteps.title: Raise Set Point
fixSteps.instruction: Increase thermostat 5 degrees above room temp to trigger a heat call.

fixSteps.title: Test with Jumper
fixSteps.instruction: Briefly jump R and W at the control board. If furnace starts, the thermostat is the issue.

whenToCallPro: If the furnace does not respond to a thermostat call or jumper test.

faq.q: Is a steady LED normal?
faq.a: Yes, it means the system is powered and ready.

faq.q: Why is the furnace not starting?
faq.a: Check your thermostat settings, mode, and batteries.

faq.q: What does blinking mean vs steady?
faq.a: Blinking indicates a fault code. Steady means normal standby.

title: No Power to Control Board
shortDescription: No LED visible — the control board is not receiving power.
fullDescription: When no LED is illuminated on the Goodman furnace control board, the board has no power. Check the breaker, power switch, board fuse, and transformer.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off the gas supply before troubleshooting electrical issues.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Find and reset the furnace breaker if tripped.

fixSteps.title: Check Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the furnace power switch is ON.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the board fuse. Replace if blown with the same amperage.

fixSteps.title: Test Transformer
fixSteps.instruction: Check for 24VAC output. No output with 120V input means failed transformer.

whenToCallPro: If the breaker trips repeatedly or the transformer has failed.

faq.q: Why does the fuse keep blowing?
faq.a: Usually a short in the thermostat wiring.

faq.q: What size fuse?
faq.a: Most Goodman furnaces use a 3A or 5A fuse.

faq.q: Can a bad thermostat blow the fuse?
faq.a: Yes, a shorted thermostat or wire can blow the board fuse.

title: Rollout Switch Open
shortDescription: The flame rollout safety switch has tripped.
fullDescription: Ten blinks on a Goodman furnace indicate the rollout limit switch opened due to flames escaping the combustion chamber. This is a serious safety condition requiring professional inspection.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately and call your gas company.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off furnace power for 30 seconds, then restore.

fixSteps.title: Check Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the air filter if dirty.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check for loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: If error persists, professional diagnosis is needed.

whenToCallPro: If basic troubleshooting does not resolve the error.

faq.q: What does 10-blink mean?
faq.a: The flame rollout safety switch has tripped.

faq.q: Is this serious?
faq.a: It depends on the specific code. Some are minor and some need immediate attention.

faq.q: How much will repair cost?
faq.a: $100-$500 depending on the issue.
